MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marianne Rossi, Esq. 650-364-7034 for Assignee Robert R.Rago (In-person)
Gina Arico-Smith, Esq. 925-817-3715 of Fidelity National Law Group for Defendant Yosemite Capital LLC.(In-person)
Court reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR #1389, 714-471-2597, justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Reported. (by Court Call)

RULING - A R G U E D. Ms. Smith argues. The Court shares its views, there is a triable issue of material fact. Ms. Rossi responds and agrees with the Court. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative as as follows:

DEFENDANT SAMSON LAI, SUSAN LAI, YOSEMITE CAPITAL, LLC, ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication.

DENIED. Every issue to be adjudicated includes facts pertaining to due diligence review of documents by Maglesini and "indication" that Wood Street Plaza, LLC and Edwin Heath were the owners. Whether documents reviewed, including the entirety of the Preliminary Report so indicated is a triable issue of fact. Additionally, as to the motion for summary adjudication of the equitable lien cause of action in the cross-complaint moving party appears to get an equitable lien only on the part of the outstanding Note amount attributable to the Ragos, which is not the cause of action alleged in the Cross-Complaint seeking a lien for the entire outstanding Note amount ("no lees than")(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Proposed order to be dropped in Dept. 501's inbox.

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Justus Balentine, CSR #1389, 714-471-2597, justusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Reported. (501/CFH)

Steven Weinstein, Esq., 510-336-2181, appearing for Mary Rago, Plaintiff.

Ryan Roe, Esq. of Fidelity National Law Group, 925-817-3715, appearing for Yosemite and Samson Lai and Susan Lai.

Hearing Held. The Court stated that there would be no preliminary injunction without the following issues addressed. 1) Is the property adequately insured? 2) Are the taxes being paid?
